output.title: California Security Deposit Recovery: Demand Letter Service | DepositBack
output.meta_description: Still waiting on your California security deposit? Create a state-specific demand letter with DepositBack and recover what's yours. Get started free.
content_outline: California Security Deposit Laws: The 21-Day Rule and Your Rights
Top Reasons California Landlords Wrongfully Withhold Deposits
How DepositBack Builds Your State-Specific Demand Letter in Minutes
What Happens After You Send Your California Demand Letter
California Security Deposit FAQs for Recently Moved-Out Renters
key_points: California landlords must return your security deposit within 21 days or provide a detailed, itemized deduction statement
Under California Civil Code § 1950.5, bad faith withholding can entitle renters to statutory damages up to twice the deposit amount
DepositBack auto-generates a California-compliant demand letter citing the exact state statutes your landlord violated
No lawyer needed—renters can create, download, and send a professional demand letter in under 5 minutes
output.cta: Generate Your California Security Deposit Demand Letter Now—It Only Takes 3 Minutes
output.target_audience: California renters who have moved out within the last 1-3 years and have not received their full security deposit back—or received unjust deductions—from their former landlord
